method.luminance_definition: black-subtracted raw Bayer values averaged over each non-overlapping 2x2 Bayer cell as (R + G1 + G2 + B) / 4, then expanded back to full resolution; no white balance, demosaic tone scaling, gamma, or human-eye weighting
method.target_detection_source: camera-rendered linear preview used only to locate the target geometry; reported values use raw-bayer-mean linear luminance
method.sigma_definition: intensity-weighted second central moments of the connected spot component after background subtraction; equals Gaussian sigma for an ideal Gaussian spot
method.encirclement_definition: aperture center is chosen to maximize integrated irradiance inside the scaled receiver aperture
method.marker_handling: printed marker dots are excluded from NRM calibration, peak search, and sigma calculation

on_image.luminance_definition: black-subtracted raw Bayer values averaged over each non-overlapping 2x2 Bayer cell as (R + G1 + G2 + B) / 4, then expanded back to full resolution; no white balance, demosaic tone scaling, gamma, or human-eye weighting
on_image.target_detection_source: camera-rendered linear preview used only to locate the target geometry; reported values use raw-bayer-mean linear luminance

off_image.luminance_definition: black-subtracted raw Bayer values averaged over each non-overlapping 2x2 Bayer cell as (R + G1 + G2 + B) / 4, then expanded back to full resolution; no white balance, demosaic tone scaling, gamma, or human-eye weighting
off_image.target_detection_source: camera-rendered linear preview used only to locate the target geometry; reported values use raw-bayer-mean linear luminance

encirclement.aperture_note: common SEC ellipse: largest shared same-aspect ellipse whose best-positioned mean is at least the same-source same-season REF aperture mean for every available SEC plot
